DECLARATION OF HAS S. JAWANDHA IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS DUCES TECUM AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 65791476) FILED BY PLAINTIFF QUICK, TYSON
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-20-2020 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE AUG-20-2020 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF TYSON QUICK'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS DUCES TECUM AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) THE COURT WILL REVIEW THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. JUDGE PRO TEM: ADRIENNE ROGERS; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-20-2019 Set For Trial On Oct-26-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Defendants Ten 15, Inc. And Elva Patinofranco
Off Calendar Dec-06-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph S. May, Esq. of the LAW OFFICE OF JOSEPH S. MAY, T:415.781.3333, for plaintiff/moving party;
Bryan Stofferahn, Esq. of BREMER WHYTE BROWN & O'MEARA LLP, T:510.540.4881 for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF TYSON QUICK's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Defendants Ten 15, Inc. And Elva Patinofranco, as follows;

Plaintiff Tyson Quick's motion for terminating sanctions against defendants Ten 15, Inc. and Elva Patinofranco is denied. First, plaintiff does not conclusively demonstrate that defense counsel filed a false declaration on purpose. The misstatement regarding the meet and confer efforts could have been the product of error. (See Stofferahn Dec.) Second, and more importantly, the requested relief far outweighs the alleged transgression. In Slesinger, Inc. v. Walt Disney Co. (2008) 155 Cal.App.4th 736, 740, the court held "that when a plaintiff's deliberate and egregious misconduct makes any sanction other than dismissal inadequate to ensure a fair trial, the trial court has inherent power to impose a terminating sanction." The court explained "that California trial courts have inherent power to issue a terminating sanction when a plaintiff's misconduct is deliberate, is egregious, and makes lesser sanctions inadequate to ensure a fair trial." (Id.) Here, the court believes that a fair trial can go forward even if the meet and confer declaration were falsified.

Order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
